origin.name: Kazakhstan
origin.key_document: Death certificate from civil registration office (requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Kazakhstan travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Astana guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Kazakhstan is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option alongside full body repatriation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Death certificate from civil registration office (certified English translation required)
special_rules: Death must be registered with the local civil registration office (ZAGS) promptly.
All Kazakh or Russian-language documentation requires certified English translation for UK acceptance.
The Prosecutor's Office orders post-mortem for suspicious or violent deaths.
An export permit from the relevant Kazakh authority is required for all repatriations.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Kazakh documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Astana can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Kazakhstan to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Kazakhstan. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-3 weeks. ZAGS registration, translation, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
